title: Manufacturers warn of a RAM memory price crisis that will last until 2028
summary: Multiple reports indicate that DRAM memory prices will continue to rise due to high demand and supply constraints, affecting the technology sector.

title: Experts point out the systemic risks of artificial intelligence for the economy and security
summary: From financial bubbles to vulnerabilities in autonomous agents, warnings about the uncontrolled use of AI are growing.

title: PlayStation 6 manufacturing costs skyrocket and Sony removes movies from accounts without refund
summary: Sony's future console would reach a production cost of $1,000, while more than 500 movies have been removed from user accounts in the United Kingdom.

title: The Steam Machine ecosystem is reborn with clones, SteamOS on pre-built PCs and a mod for Nintendo Switch
summary: Several manufacturers launch devices compatible with SteamOS, while a modder manages to run the interface on a Switch.

title: California bans excessively loud ads on streaming services
summary: The new California law will take effect on July 1 and will require platforms to standardize advertising volumes.

title: Ford rehires experienced engineers after confirming that AI cannot replace them
summary: The automotive company acknowledges that artificial intelligence models cannot replace the experience of veteran engineers.

title: Multiple cases of GeForce RTX 5090 with melted connector and denied warranty
summary: Users report that the NVIDIA graphics card suffers serious damage to the 16-pin connector and manufacturers refuse to cover the damage.

title: China develops AI tools for cybersecurity on par with Anthropic
summary: Chinese companies present artificial intelligence systems that directly compete with Anthropic's cybersecurity solutions.

title: Online age verification laws advance amid criticism over their impact on privacy
summary: The KIDS Act and other legislative proposals seek to impose age controls, but organizations warn that they may harm free speech and anonymity.

title: SpaceX reaches millions of retirement funds thanks to changes in stock indices
summary: SpaceX shares enter 401(k) plans after adjustments to index rules, also opening the door to OpenAI and Anthropic.

title: Microsoft, which once called Linux 'cancer', launches its own free distribution different from Ubuntu or Fedora
summary: The company made a radical turn and now distributes its own Linux system, marking a milestone in its relationship with open source.

title: The rise of data centers in the US generates social backlash that puts the AI boom at risk
summary: More and more communities oppose the installation of data centers due to their energy consumption and environmental impact.

title: New supercomputer leads the TOP500 list at ISC'26 conference
summary: The TOP500 presents a new number one, marking a significant advance in high-performance computing.

title: Global warming is accelerating at an unprecedented rate, scientists warn
summary: Climate indicators show an accelerated rise in global temperatures, approaching the point of no return.

title: SoftBank CEO defends investment in AI: 'Saying it's a bubble is blasphemy'
summary: Masayoshi Son assures that the current boom in artificial intelligence is only the beginning and denies it is a bubble.

title: Professor reports massive AI fraud in a Brown University exam
summary: Widespread use of artificial intelligence to cheat on an academic test was detected, generating an ethical debate.

title: School buses in the US will become mobile surveillance vehicles
summary: New technologies will turn the buses into surveillance units that will collect route and behavior data.

title: TechCrunch Mobility: All eyes on Tesla's Full Self-Driving system
summary: The TechCrunch Mobility newsletter analyzes the latest developments of Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) and its impact on the industry.

title: Big tech's $8 trillion bet on AI makes consoles, cars and electricity more expensive
summary: Massive investment in artificial intelligence is raising costs in other sectors such as video games and automotive.

title: Canada successfully replaces steam generators in a nuclear reactor while on the go
summary: In an exceptional operation, eight 100-ton generators were removed from the reactor and new ones installed, advancing the restart by seven months.
